title: `readinto` return value unchecked in VFD driver
author: devdanzin
body:
Partial reads leave buffer uninitialized (data corruption)
- `h5fd.templ.pyx:156-168`

comments.author: neutrinoceros
comments.body:
I'm confused by this one.
It looks to me that the problematic path is `return 1`, which *might* be how the function signals failure (although I'm being pretty liberal with my interpretation of [`herr_t`'s docs](https://portal.hdfgroup.org/documentation/hdf5/latest/_h5public_8h.html#a3b079ecf932a5c599499cf7e298af160)), so I think the *caller* would be expected to check the return value before using the buffer ?
It would seem more in line with `herr_t`'s conventions to return -1 instead, or possibly some other negative value.
Besides, the function  uses [`except -1`](https://docs.cython.org/en/latest/src/userguide/language_basics.html#error-return-values) which is unsettling since it never returns this value... maybe the original intention was for this path to raise (`return 1`), which would have avoided the defect entirely
